Name: quantized_neural_networks_with_risk_measures
Title: Quantized Neural Networks with Risk Measures: An Information-Theoretic Approach
Short Hypothesis: We hypothesize that by incorporating risk measures into the quantization process, we can develop more efficient and robust quantized neural networks that balance the trade-off between compression and distortion. This approach will allow us to derive achievable rate-distortion bounds and converse bounds for quantized neural networks, providing a fundamental understanding of the limits of compression.
Related Work: Previous work has focused on developing quantization methods for neural networks, but these methods often rely on simplistic distortion measures that do not account for the uncertainty in the distortion function. Recently, goal-oriented 1-bit quantization with uncertain distortion measures has been proposed, but this work does not provide a comprehensive information-theoretic framework for quantized neural networks. Our approach builds upon this work and provides a more general framework for quantizing neural networks with risk measures, drawing inspiration from the principles of information-theoretic limits and rate-distortion theory.
Abstract: This paper proposes a novel approach to quantizing neural networks using risk measures, which provides a more robust and efficient way to balance the trade-off between compression and distortion. We derive achievable rate-distortion bounds and converse bounds for quantized neural networks, providing a fundamental understanding of the limits of compression. Our approach is based on the principle of minimizing the risk of distortion, rather than the distortion itself, and is inspired by the principles of information-theoretic limits and rate-distortion theory. We demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach through experiments on benchmark datasets, showing that our method outperforms existing quantization methods in terms of compression ratio and distortion.
Experiments: We will conduct experiments on benchmark datasets, such as ImageNet and CIFAR-10, to evaluate the performance of our approach. We will compare our method to existing quantization methods, such as uniform quantization and entropy-constrained quantization, in terms of compression ratio and distortion. We will also evaluate the robustness of our approach to different types of noise and distortions, and investigate the trade-off between compression and distortion using risk measures.
Risk Factors and Limitations: One potential risk factor is that our approach may not be able to capture the complex relationships between the input data and the distortion function, which could lead to suboptimal performance. Another limitation is that our approach requires a good estimate of the risk measure, which can be challenging to obtain in practice. Additionally, our approach may not be suitable for very large neural networks, due to the computational complexity of the risk measure estimation.
